# NOMAD — MASTER SCRIPT

**Rendered from MASTER DRAFT v0.8. Do not edit — changes go to the draft; this re-renders.**

---

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — DAY

SUPER: ONE YEAR AGO.

An office at its midpoint. Twenty people scattered among visibly recent emptiness. Nobody looks up.

CAIRNS (30s, loud) packs his desk into a printer-paper box, narrating it to a floor that isn't listening. Only WILL and JOHN watch. One polite chuckle from somewhere. [[The exit speech plays only to Will and John — that's the ache: a performance for an audience that already left.]]

A ROBOT BOSS — a monitor on a wheeled stand, running the boss's warm AI likeness — rolls up. The three of them banter with it. It answers a half-beat late, every time. [[First planting of the 0.4-second delay. It pays off twice more.]]

Cairns says the thing he means for the real boss.

INT. BOSS'S HOUSE — CONTINUOUS

The real boss, mid golf-sim swing, the office feed propped on a phone he isn't watching.

INT. OFFICE SPACE — CONTINUOUS

The likeness thanks Cairns warmly for the feedback.

## INT. ELEVATOR BANK — CONTINUOUS

Will and John flank Cairns, carrying nothing. John asks the plan. Cairns brags about a SERVICE — handles everything, one fee — and can't answer a single follow-up. [[The Service exists in his mouth. A person named Michelle never does.]]

The doors close on him mid-sentence.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — MOMENTS LATER

The floor, back at work. Cairns' chair still spinning. On the desk: a branded welcome folder.

SUPER: ONE YEAR LATER.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — MORNING

The same floor. Nine people. Motion-sensor lights click on ahead of Will in sequence — a runway for nobody. He passes dark departments, and Cairns' untouched desk, the folder gathering a year of dust.

At 8:55 the coffee machine brews a full pot. Will pours its only cup.

Somewhere in the dark, the Robot Boss greets empty desks by name.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — WILL'S DESK — DAY

A queue of faces. Machine verdicts. One button: APPROVE. Will clicks. A streak counter climbs.

A face, machine-verified 98%. Will stops. Replays three seconds — asked about his childhood, the man's eyes go nowhere. Will overrides: SYNTHETIC. The system concedes. His streak resets to zero. [[The tell — eyes that don't remember. Taught here at desk scale so it can pay off on a 40-foot screen.]]

He looks up to tell someone. Eight heads down.

On his monitor: SYSTEM 99.1 / WILL 96.8 — TOP HUMAN PERFORMER.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

Dinner over the sink. An HR popup — 34 unused vacation days — dismissed by reflex. [[Loneliness as efficiency. No music, no rain. Don't be corny.]]

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — WILL'S DESK — DAY

Will's display: CAIRNS, poolside, sun, abundance.

CAIRNS
You wouldn't believe it here, bro. Everything's two for one. And I'm not just talking about the food — right, ladies?

TWO GIRLS
¡Sí!

CAIRNS
You gotta get out here. It's life on crack. Same setup for work — but with bottomless margaritas.

The girls escalate — decoration, distraction, pulling him from frame.

CAIRNS
Anyway — you know I'm tight with Michelle. Say the word, I'll get you set up.

CAIRNS
Ladies, ladies. Plenty of papi to go around. Ladies!

They drag him off.

CAIRNS (O.S.)
Love you, buddy!

The screen stays on. The three run to the pool. Cairns throws one girl in. Will watches — a tinge of jealousy in it.

JOHN (O.S.)
Is that Cairns? Nice.

Will kills the tab and turns. John, holding two beers.

## INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT — MINI-PUTT COURSE — LATER

One round by phone-light. Hole 7 uses the old CEO's office door as a ramp.

JOHN
Doesn't sound too bad. You know I've never even left the state?

They let it sit. Then — who's Michelle? The Service was a company; since when does it have a first name? Neither of them knows. They shrug it off. [[Michelle enters the film as a hole in Cairns' story. Da fuq is Michelle.]]

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — WILL'S DESK — DAY

The dashboard: the machine's decisions scrolling too fast to read. Will spots a fraud he's sure of. INTERVENE. His screen dims — a coaching module auto-plays, "Trusting Your Tools," a cheerful presenter who blinks at perfectly regular intervals. In the corner, the system clears his case without him. [[The presenter's regular blink is never mentioned. Some of the audience catches it. That's the right number.]]

## INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT — DAY

Mini-putt with John. Mid-round, Will checks the time — and just leaves. Mid-afternoon. Nobody stops him. Nobody notices.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

The pool clip, rewatched with the sound off. He closes the laptop. Opens it. Looks up the Service.

## INT. SERVICE STOREFRONT — DAY

An office park. The Service sits between a vape shop and a notary. Brochure rack, folding chairs, a paradise reel looping over a dying pothos. A bored coordinator checks Will in like a dentist's office. [[Scams don't have lobbies. Franchises do. The dinginess is what makes it believable.]]

INSERT — a scanner's green line crosses his passport.

Face capture: look left, look right, read this sentence aloud. Will jokes that he does this for a living.

COORDINATOR
Then you know it's painless.

A half-beat late.

## INT. WILL'S CAR — PARKING LOT — CONTINUOUS

The email arrives before he's out of the lot. MICHELLE — template-warm, exclamation points, a scheduling link.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

Michelle's welcome preview: an automated ad starring WILL — his face, his walk, composited into Cairns' paradise. "See yourself there." He watches himself live the ad, delighted.

INSERT — a checkbox fills: CONFIRM MY DETAILS.

He says his name for the personalization. Says it again, excited. Grinning. [[The ad is the trap, and now he's inside it. Also plants the generative tech for the render farm.]]

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — LATER

The tour quiz: an app films him walking the apartment, narrating furniture. For confirmation it plays his face back to him — and for half a second his own eyes look wrong. He blames the compression. [[The tell, planted a second time — on Will himself.]]

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — DAY

A bloodless email: THE FLOOR IS CLOSING. Full remote by end of October.

John's answer: the last day in the office is the MINI-PUTT CHAMPIONSHIP. He starts the bracket.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

John helps Will pack. He tapes WILL on the grip of a putter and keeps it.

JOHN
So you don't have an excuse.

Will promises to be back for it, half-listening. [[The putter is the anchor object. It does the film's grieving in the last shot.]]

## EXT. MEXICO CITY — DAY

The ad comes true. The apartment beats the photos. The fridge is stocked with his exact brands. A rooftop workday; Will closes the laptop mid-refresh. No clocks anywhere. [[Paradise abolishes time. Michelle's appointment will be the first specific time he's given in the country.]]

He texts Cairns on landing. "Next week, bro." Invites him to a party. "Just left town."

A rooftop party recreates the frame of Cairns' call — someone thrown in the pool. Will catches himself inside the ad, and toasts it. He films it for Cairns. The reply is warm, instant, and postpones again.

He passes a conversation between locals — subtitled for us, not for him — about the building where the American who's never home lives. The building sits behind them, unnoticed. [[The audience gets there an act before Will does.]]

## INT. WILL'S CDMX APARTMENT / INT. DEAD OFFICE — NIGHT — INTERCUT

Will calls JOHN. Mini-putt updates from the dark floor; Will poolside, pretending he isn't lonely at a party.

JOHN
Weird — Cairns liked my bracket post yesterday. He's clearly online.

[[The one honest phone call in the film. It's what scene 9's fake gets measured against.]]

## INT. WILL'S CDMX APARTMENT — NIGHT

The poolside frame. Cairns, still selling, still "next week." Will, easy and warm, asks a nothing question — a private memory only the two of them hold.

The answer comes back correct but transcribed. Facts right. Eyes going nowhere.

Four seconds of Will fully awake. And the call does not end. It goes on, pleasantly — Will performing normalcy, managing a goodnight.

CAIRNS
Love you, bro.

A half-beat late. Will closes the laptop and sits very still in the best apartment he's ever had. [[The horror is that nothing happens.]]

## INT. WILL'S CDMX APARTMENT — NIGHT — THE SCRUB

Desk lamp. Side-by-sides: years of real Cairns — birthdays, dumb ones — frame-stepped against the new calls. Timestamps across time zones that don't add up. The laugh, isolated and waveformed: a half-second late. [[Fraud ops turned all the way inward. Same method as his desk, same verdict.]]

The phone lights. MICHELLE: the final appointment, moved up. Tomorrow. A time — the first he's been given here. An address he half-knows.

## EXT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — DAY

Across the street. Ordinary. He watches it a while.

## INT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — DAY

A day early. Reception expects him tomorrow; nobody manages what he sees. The service stairs.

Upstairs: nothing. No set, no pool. An empty floor — chairs, capture rigs like the storefront's, racks of humming servers.

Through a doorway: the RENDER FARM. An operator types a line and a poolside evening assembles in seconds — Cairns' pool, water that has never been wet. On another screen, a girl who doesn't exist is thrown laughing into it; a slider drags her laugh warmer. On a third: familiar faces, likenesses at work, attending standups.

On one screen, mid-generation: WILL'S OWN welcome ad, refining. Him, poolside, saying things he's never said.

From the next row — WILL'S OWN LAUGH. The real one. He follows his own voice. [[The devastation arrives by sound before image.]]

The worst screen in the building: CAIRNS' LIKENESS on a call with WILL'S LIKENESS. Poolside together. Their in-jokes. The friendship, automated, running fine without either of them. Version numbers in the corner. [[Not that he was copied — that the thing he crossed a border for is happening on a server, and neither of them is in it.]]

Will slips out. An operator clocks him in a doorway reflection, and doesn't care.

## THE DOUBLE RITUAL — INTERCUT

EXT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — DAY

From below. Monolithic. It fills the frame. Will walks in. [[Third look at the building — bigger each time.]]

INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT — DAY

LAST DAY. The floor half-dismantled — desks tagged, cables coiled, the coffee machine unplugged. A paper banner goes up. The bracket: one name crossed out, rewritten smaller — WILL (REMOTE?).

INT. CLEAN ROOM — DAY

A chair, good light, liveness rigs he knows from his own firm. Forms already complete.

INSERT — a checkbox, already filled: REFERENCE RETENTION.

Behind him, a wall of small numbered lockers, most tagged. One readable: a first name and a version number. [[The rule — originals are kept — stated silently, as furniture.]]

INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT

John in a homemade referee shirt by hole 7. Two beers. Will's putter, WILL on the grip, leans against the hole. Reserved.

INT. CLEAN ROOM

The door closes.

INT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — HALLWAY

Will's phone, face-up on a tray. Michelle's thread archiving itself. The screen sleeps. [[The last we see of the real Will. He is captured — kept.]]

INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT

John's phone BUZZES.

A message from Will — cadence perfect, warmth a shade too on-message: not gonna make it back, bro. But Mexico's crazy. You gotta come out HERE.

We never see the screen. Only John's face, lit by it — a flicker of disappointment, then delight.

John smiles. Starts typing back.

Hold on John. The putter in frame.

One frame stutters.

CUT TO BLACK.

THE END
